# Forum Project — Team Roadmap (4 Members)

This roadmap follows a **Server-Side Rendering (SSR)** architecture using Go's standard library and `html/template`. The browser receives fully rendered HTML pages from the Go server, while forms submit directly to the backend using standard HTTP requests.

Unlike a Single Page Application (SPA), the application does **not** rely on JavaScript `fetch()` calls or JSON APIs for its core functionality. This approach aligns closely with the project requirements, emphasizing HTTP, sessions, cookies, SQLite, templates, and proper server-side error handling.

Development is organized into **9 phases**. Each phase is divided into **4 parallel tasks (A/B/C/D)** so every team member has clear ownership while minimizing merge conflicts.

---

# Development Order

* Phase 1 — HTTP Server *(runs in parallel with Phase 2)*
* Phase 2 — HTML Templates *(runs in parallel with Phase 1)*
* Phase 3 — Database
* Phase 4 — Authentication
* Phase 5 — Posts
* Phase 6 — Comments
* Phase 7 — Likes & Dislikes
* Phase 8 — Filters
* Phase 9 — Testing, Docker & Final Polish

From **Phase 3 onward**, each phase depends on the previous one.

---

# Target Project Structure

```text
forum/
├── go.mod
├── go.sum
├── Dockerfile
├── docker-compose.yml
├── README.md
│
├── cmd/
│   └── server/
│       └── main.go
│
├── internal/
│   ├── router/
│   │   └── router.go
│   │
│   ├── middleware/
│   │   └── middleware.go
│   │
│   ├── db/
│   │   ├── db.go
│   │   └── schema.sql
│   │
│   ├── auth/
│   │   ├── password.go
│   │   └── session.go
│   │
│   ├── handlers/
│   │   ├── home.go
│   │   ├── auth.go
│   │   ├── posts.go
│   │   ├── comments.go
│   │   ├── reactions.go
│   │   ├── filters.go
│   │   └── errors.go
│   │
│   └── models/
│       ├── user.go
│       ├── category.go
│       ├── post.go
│       ├── comment.go
│       └── reaction.go
│
├── web/
│   ├── templates/
│   │   ├── base.html
│   │   ├── index.html
│   │   ├── login.html
│   │   ├── register.html
│   │   ├── new-post.html
│   │   ├── post.html
│   │   ├── category.html
│   │   ├── profile.html
│   │   ├── 404.html
│   │   └── 500.html
│   │
│   └── static/
│       ├── css/
│       │   └── style.css
│       ├── js/
│       │   └── main.js
│       └── images/
│
└── tests/
```

---

# Phase 1 — HTTP Server *(runs in parallel with Phase 2)*

## Goal

Create the application foundation.

At the end of this phase:

* Go server runs.
* Static assets load.
* Templates can be rendered.
* Logging works.
* Graceful shutdown works.
* Custom 404 page works.

| Owner | Task                                                                                                                                               |
| ----- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **A** | Create `cmd/server/main.go`. Configure the HTTP server, graceful shutdown (`SIGINT`, `SIGTERM`), and application startup.                          |
| **B** | Create `internal/router/router.go`. Configure the router, register routes, serve static assets from `/static/`, and implement custom 404 handling. |
| **C** | Create `internal/middleware/middleware.go`. Implement request logging middleware (method, path, status code, duration).                            |
| **D** | Create the initial `Dockerfile` and `README.md` so everyone can build and run the application from day one.                                        |

### Initial Routes

```text
GET /
GET /login
GET /register
GET /new-post
GET /profile
```

These routes may render placeholder templates until later phases.

### Test Before Merge

* `go run ./cmd/server`
* `/` returns HTTP 200.
* Unknown routes return custom HTTP 404.
* Static CSS loads correctly.
* Logging displays request details.
* Server shuts down cleanly.

---

# Phase 2 — HTML Templates *(runs in parallel with Phase 1)*

## Goal

Build every page of the forum using Go templates.

At this stage the pages still use placeholder data.

Create a shared template layout using:

* `base.html`
* reusable navigation
* reusable footer

Every page should inherit the common layout.

| Owner | Task                                                                                                                                                                                |
| ----- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **A** | Build `base.html`, shared navigation, footer, global `style.css`, and `index.html`. Create placeholder post cards showing title, author, categories, likes, dislikes, and comments. |
| **B** | Build `login.html` and `register.html`. Create complete HTML forms using standard `<form>` elements (no JavaScript). Reserve template blocks for validation errors.                 |
| **C** | Build `new-post.html` and `post.html`. Include post form, category selection, comment section, and like/dislike forms using placeholder data.                                       |
| **D** | Build `category.html` and `profile.html`. Profile should include "My Posts" and "Liked Posts" sections using mock data.                                                             |

### Template Goals

Prepare templates for future dynamic data using Go template syntax such as:

* `{{.User}}`
* `{{range .Posts}}`
* `{{if .LoggedIn}}`

Only placeholder data is required during this phase.

### Test Before Merge

* Every page renders.
* Navigation works.
* Responsive layout.
* Consistent styling.
* No broken links.

### Team Review

Before starting Phase 3, review every page together.

Decide:

* What information belongs to a post?
* What information belongs to a comment?
* Which categories should exist?
* Which statistics should appear on every page?

These decisions determine the database schema.

---

# Phase 3 — SQLite Database

## Goal

Create the persistent data layer.

The database must support:

* Users
* Sessions
* Categories
* Posts
* Comments
* Reactions

### Suggested Tables

* users
* sessions
* posts
* categories
* post_categories
* comments
* reactions

| Owner | Task                                                                                                                                                      |
| ----- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **A** | Design the ERD and create `internal/db/schema.sql`. Define tables, foreign keys, indexes, and unique constraints.                                         |
| **B** | Create `internal/db/db.go`. Configure SQLite (`foreign_keys=ON`, WAL mode, busy timeout), initialize the database, and execute migrations automatically.  |
| **C** | Create `internal/models/category.go`. Seed the default forum categories and implement repository methods for retrieving categories.                       |
| **D** | Create `internal/models/user.go`. Implement user creation, lookup by email, lookup by username, and basic repository methods required for authentication. |

### Test Before Merge

* Database file is created.
* Tables exist.
* Categories are seeded.
* User insertion works.
* Duplicate email is rejected.
* Foreign keys are enforced.

### Team Review

Before Phase 4, verify that the schema supports:

* multiple categories per post
* comments
* likes/dislikes
* sessions
* user ownership
* future filtering without major schema changes
# Phase 4 — Authentication

## Goal

Implement complete user authentication using server-side sessions and cookies.

The authentication system must support:

* User registration.
* User login.
* User logout.
* Session creation.
* Session expiration.
* Protected routes.
* Password encryption using bcrypt.

All authentication is handled by Go. No JavaScript authentication requests are used.

The browser submits normal HTML forms:

```html
<form action="/login" method="POST">
```

The server validates the request, updates the database, sets cookies, and redirects the user.

---

## Authentication Flow

### Registration

```text
User fills register form

        ↓

POST /register

        ↓

Validate input

        ↓

Check email exists

        ↓

Hash password

        ↓

Save user

        ↓

Redirect to login
```

---

### Login

```text
User submits login form

        ↓

POST /login

        ↓

Check credentials

        ↓

Create session

        ↓

Set cookie

        ↓

Redirect to homepage
```

---

## Routes

```text
GET  /register
POST /register

GET  /login
POST /login

POST /logout
```

---

| Owner | Task                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| ----- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **A** | Implement `internal/auth/password.go`. Add bcrypt password hashing and password comparison helpers. Extend `internal/models/user.go` with authentication-related queries. Implement registration handler logic in `internal/handlers/auth.go`. |
| **B** | Implement `internal/auth/session.go`. Create session management using UUID session IDs, expiration dates, secure cookies, and session database storage. Implement login/logout handlers.                                                       |
| **C** | Extend `internal/middleware/middleware.go`. Create authentication middleware that reads the session cookie, validates it, loads the current user, and attaches user data to the request context. Protect routes requiring authentication.      |
| **D** | Update templates (`login.html`, `register.html`, navigation). Add server-rendered validation messages, logged-in username display, and logout links.                                                                                           |

---

## Template Changes

Navigation should dynamically display:

When logged out:

```text
Login
Register
```

When logged in:

```text
Username
Profile
Logout
```

Templates should support server-side messages:

```html
{{if .Error}}
<div class="error">
    {{.Error}}
</div>
{{end}}
```

---

## Test Before Merge

* Register a new account successfully.
* Register with an existing email returns an error.
* Login with incorrect password returns an error.
* Login creates a session cookie.
* Protected pages reject unauthenticated users.
* Logout removes the session.
* Expired sessions are rejected.

---

# Phase 5 — Posts & Categories

## Goal

Allow users to create posts with categories while allowing everyone to view posts.

Requirements covered:

* Registered users can create posts.
* Posts can have multiple categories.
* Guests can view posts.
* Categories are displayed with posts.
* Posts belong to their creator.

No API endpoints are used. All pages are rendered by Go templates.

---

## Post Creation Flow

```text
User opens new post page

        ↓

GET /new-post

        ↓

Fill form

        ↓

POST /new-post

        ↓

Validate session

        ↓

Save post

        ↓

Attach categories

        ↓

Redirect to post page
```

---

## Routes

```text
GET  /
GET  /post?id={id}

GET  /new-post
POST /new-post
```

---

| Owner | Task                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| ----- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **A** | Implement `internal/models/post.go`. Create post creation queries, post retrieval, category relationships, and feed queries.                                                              |
| **B** | Implement post handlers in `internal/handlers/posts.go`. Create homepage rendering, post detail rendering, and post creation processing.                                                  |
| **C** | Update templates. Implement dynamic post rendering using Go templates: `index.html`, `post.html`, and `new-post.html`. Add template loops for posts, categories, comments, and reactions. |
| **D** | Implement validation and integration testing. Verify ownership checks, category assignment, empty input handling, and correct redirect behaviour after post creation.                     |

---

## Database Operations

Post creation requires:

1. Insert post.

Example:

```sql
INSERT INTO posts (...)
VALUES (...);
```

2. Insert category relationships.

Example:

```sql
INSERT INTO post_categories (...)
VALUES (...);
```

3. Retrieve posts with category information.

Example:

```sql
SELECT posts.*, categories.name
FROM posts
JOIN post_categories
ON posts.id = post_categories.post_id;
```

---

## Template Example

Posts are rendered server-side:

```html
{{range .Posts}}

<article>

<h2>{{.Title}}</h2>

<p>
Created by {{.Username}}
</p>

{{range .Categories}}
<span>
{{.Name}}
</span>
{{end}}

</article>

{{end}}
```

---

## Test Before Merge

* Guest users can view posts.
* Logged-in users can create posts.
* Logged-out users cannot create posts.
* A post can contain multiple categories.
* Post detail pages display correct information.
* Homepage shows latest posts.

---

# Phase 6 — Comments

## Goal

Implement communication between users through comments.

Requirements covered:

* Registered users can comment.
* Guests can read comments.
* Comments belong to posts.
* Comment counts are displayed.

---

## Comment Flow

```text
User opens post page

        ↓

Reads comments

        ↓

Submits comment form

        ↓

POST /comment

        ↓

Validate session

        ↓

Save comment

        ↓

Redirect back to post
```

---

## Routes

```text
GET  /post?id={id}

POST /comment
```

---

| Owner | Task                                                                                                                                         |
| ----- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **A** | Implement `internal/models/comment.go`. Add create comment, list comments by post, and comment count queries.                                |
| **B** | Implement `internal/handlers/comments.go`. Handle comment submissions, authentication checks, validation, and redirects.                     |
| **C** | Update `post.html` template. Add server-rendered comment list and comment submission form. Display login message when guests cannot comment. |
| **D** | Integration testing. Verify comment ownership, comment counts, permissions, empty comment handling, and display consistency across pages.    |

---

## Template Behaviour

Logged-in user:

```html
<form action="/comment" method="POST">

<textarea name="content"></textarea>

<button>
Comment
</button>

</form>
```

Guest:

```html
<p>
Login to comment
</p>
```

---

## Test Before Merge

* Registered users can add comments.
* Guests can view comments.
* Guests cannot submit comments.
* New comments appear after redirect.
* Comment counts match database values.
* Invalid empty comments are rejected.

---

# Phase 6 Completion Check

At this point the forum should support:

 User registration
 User login
 User sessions
 User logout
 Creating posts
 Assigning categories
 Viewing posts
 Creating comments
 Viewing comments

The remaining phases will add:

* Likes/dislikes.
* Filtering.
* Error handling.
* Testing.
* Docker deployment.
# Phase 7 — Likes & Dislikes

## Goal

Implement reactions on posts and comments.

Requirements covered:

* Registered users can like posts.
* Registered users can dislike posts.
* Registered users can like comments.
* Registered users can dislike comments.
* Guests can view reaction counts.
* Each user can have only one reaction per post/comment.
* Reaction state persists after refresh.

The implementation uses normal HTML forms and server-side processing.

No JavaScript updates are required.

---

## Reaction Flow

```text
User clicks like/dislike

        ↓

POST /reaction

        ↓

Check authentication

        ↓

Check existing reaction

        ↓

Create/update/remove reaction

        ↓

Redirect back

        ↓

Page reloads with updated count
```

---

## Routes

```text
POST /reaction
```

The request contains:

* target type (post/comment)
* target ID
* reaction type (like/dislike)

Example form:

```html
<form action="/reaction" method="POST">

<input type="hidden" name="post_id" value="10">

<button name="type" value="like">
Like
</button>

<button name="type" value="dislike">
Dislike
</button>

</form>
```

---

| Owner | Task                                                                                                                                                        |
| ----- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **A** | Implement `internal/models/reaction.go`. Create reaction queries, unique constraints, insert/update/remove logic, and count queries for likes and dislikes. |
| **B** | Implement `internal/handlers/reactions.go`. Handle reaction submissions, authentication checks, validation, and redirects back to the previous page.        |
| **C** | Update templates (`post.html`, comment sections, feed cards). Add like/dislike forms and display reaction counts using template variables.                  |
| **D** | Integration testing. Verify reactions from multiple users, reaction switching, persistence after refresh, and visibility for guests.                        |

---

## Database Logic

A user should not be able to create duplicate reactions.

The database should enforce this:

```sql
UNIQUE(user_id, post_id)
```

or

```sql
UNIQUE(user_id, comment_id)
```

---

## Reaction Behaviour

Example:

User likes a post:

```text
Like count +1
```

User changes to dislike:

```text
Like count -1
Dislike count +1
```

User removes reaction:

```text
Dislike count -1
```

---

## Test Before Merge

* Logged-in user can like posts.
* Logged-in user can dislike posts.
* Logged-in user can react to comments.
* A user cannot have both like and dislike active.
* Clicking the same reaction removes it.
* Guest users cannot react.
* Counts remain correct after page refresh.
* Different users have independent reactions.

---

# Phase 8 — Filters

## Goal

Implement server-side filtering of forum content.

Requirements covered:

Users must be able to filter posts by:

* Categories.
* Their own created posts.
* Posts they liked.

The last two options require authentication.

All filtering happens in Go before templates are rendered.

---

## Filter Flow

```text
User clicks filter link

        ↓

GET request with query parameter

        ↓

Handler reads filter

        ↓

Database query executes

        ↓

Template renders filtered posts
```

---

## Routes

Category filter:

```text
GET /?category={id}
```

User posts:

```text
GET /profile?filter=mine
```

Liked posts:

```text
GET /profile?filter=liked
```

---

| Owner | Task                                                                                                                                                |
| ----- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **A** | Implement category filtering queries in `internal/models/post.go` or `internal/handlers/filters.go`. Return posts belonging to selected categories. |
| **B** | Implement "My Posts" filtering. Retrieve only posts created by the currently logged-in user. Add authentication checks.                             |
| **C** | Implement "Liked Posts" filtering. Query reactions table and return posts liked by the current user.                                                |
| **D** | Update templates and navigation. Add category links, profile filtering links, and server-rendered filtered views.                                   |

---

## Template Examples

Category navigation:

```html
<a href="/?category=1">
Technology
</a>
```

Profile filters:

```html
<a href="/profile?filter=mine">
My Posts
</a>

<a href="/profile?filter=liked">
Liked Posts
</a>
```

---

## Test Before Merge

* Category filter only displays matching posts.
* Guest users cannot access personal filters.
* "My Posts" only shows the current user's posts.
* "Liked Posts" only shows posts liked by the current user.
* Two different accounts cannot see each other's private filters.

---

# Phase 9 — Error Handling, Testing, Docker & Final Polish

## Goal

Prepare the project for final submission.

This phase ensures:

* Correct HTTP behaviour.
* Stable deployment.
* Good code quality.
* Automated tests.
* Docker compatibility.

---

# HTTP Error Handling

All handlers must correctly handle:

| Status | Usage                                     |
| ------ | ----------------------------------------- |
| 200    | Successful request                        |
| 302    | Redirect after successful form submission |
| 400    | Invalid user input                        |
| 401    | Not authenticated                         |
| 403    | Forbidden action                          |
| 404    | Resource not found                        |
| 405    | Wrong HTTP method                         |
| 500    | Internal server error                     |

---

## Error Pages

Create:

```text
web/templates/

404.html
500.html
```

Example:

Request:

```text
GET /post?id=999
```

If the post does not exist:

```text
HTTP/1.1 404 Not Found
```

Render:

```text
Post not found
```

---

| Owner | Task                                                                                                                                                                            |
| ----- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **A** | Audit every handler. Verify correct HTTP status codes, validation errors, missing resources, forbidden actions, and unexpected errors. Implement shared error handling helpers. |
| **B** | Create unit tests. Test models, database operations, password hashing, sessions, and authentication logic.                                                                      |
| **C** | Complete Docker setup. Create production Dockerfile, docker-compose configuration, SQLite volume persistence, and verify container builds.                                      |
| **D** | Complete README documentation and perform final manual QA across the entire application. Test every feature and every error case.                                               |

---

# Testing Requirements

## Automated Tests

Run:

```bash
go test ./...
```

Tests should cover:

### Models

* User creation.
* User lookup.
* Post creation.
* Category relationships.
* Comment creation.
* Reaction handling.

### Authentication

* Password hashing.
* Password comparison.
* Session creation.
* Session expiration.

---

# Docker Requirements

The final application must run inside Docker.

Required:

* Multi-stage Docker build.
* SQLite database persistence.
* Application starts automatically.
* Data survives container restart.

Example:

```bash
docker compose up
```

---

# Final Manual QA Checklist

## Authentication

* [ ] Register works.
* [ ] Duplicate email rejected.
* [ ] Login works.
* [ ] Wrong password rejected.
* [ ] Logout works.
* [ ] Session expiration works.

## Posts

* [ ] Users can create posts.
* [ ] Guests can view posts.
* [ ] Categories display correctly.

## Comments

* [ ] Users can comment.
* [ ] Guests can view comments.
* [ ] Comment counts are correct.

## Reactions

* [ ] Likes work.
* [ ] Dislikes work.
* [ ] Switching reactions works.
* [ ] Counts persist.

## Filters

* [ ] Category filtering works.
* [ ] My posts filter works.
* [ ] Liked posts filter works.

## Technical

* [ ] HTTP status codes are correct.
* [ ] Errors have proper pages.
* [ ] Docker works.
* [ ] Database persists.
* [ ] Tests pass.

---

# Final Project Architecture

The completed application follows:

```text
Browser
   |
   | HTTP Request
   |
Go Router
   |
Middleware
   |
Handlers
   |
Models
   |
SQLite Database
   |
Templates
   |
Rendered HTML
   |
Browser
```

The final project demonstrates:

* HTML
* HTTP
* Cookies
* Sessions
* SQLite
* SQL queries
* Authentication
* Encryption
* Docker
* Server-side rendering
* Good Go practices

---

# Completion Criteria

The forum is complete when:

 Users can register and login
 Sessions use cookies
 Passwords are encrypted
 Users can create posts
 Posts have categories
 Users can comment
 Users can like/dislike posts and comments
 Users can filter posts
 SQLite stores all data
 HTTP errors are handled correctly
 Docker deployment works
 Tests pass
